title: Isaiah 45
subtitle: Cyrus: Anointed for God’s Purpose

sections.header: Verses 1–13 — “I Summon You by Name Though You Do Not Acknowledge Me”

heb.paragraph: "This is what the LORD says to his anointed (māshîḥa), to Cyrus, whose right hand I take hold of to subdue nations before him" (v.1). Cyrus is called māshîḥa — MESSIAH. The only non-Israelite in the entire Bible to receive this title. "I summon you by name and bestow on you a title of honour, though you do not acknowledge me" (v.4). God uses people who don’t know him for purposes they don’t understand. "I am the LORD, and there is no other; apart from me there is no God. I will strengthen you, though you have not acknowledged me" (v.5). "I form the light and create darkness, I bring prosperity and create disaster; I, the LORD, do all these things" (v.7). The most comprehensive sovereignty claim in the Bible: light AND darkness, prosperity AND disaster.

notes.note: "To his anointed, to Cyrus." A pagan king called Messiah. The title reserved for Davidic kings given to a Persian emperor. The theological scandal is deliberate: God’s purposes exceed Israel’s categories.

"I summon you by name, though you do not acknowledge me." The God who names stars (40:26) now names a king. Cyrus doesn’t know YHWH; YHWH knows Cyrus. Sovereignty operates through unknowing instruments.

"I form the light and create darkness, I bring prosperity and create disaster." The most absolute sovereignty statement in the Bible. No dualism: good and evil both under God’s hand. This does not make God the author of sin but refuses to exempt him from governance over all that happens.

refs.note: Daniel prospered under Cyrus — historical confirmation that the Persian king favored YHWH's people.
The Cyrus decree's details — temple dimensions, cost from royal treasury — show the prophecy's concrete fulfilment.
The earlier 'one from the east' oracle now finds its explicit referent in Cyrus.
Every knee bowing and tongue confessing derives from Isaiah 45:23, applied Christologically by Paul.

hist.context: Chapter 45 contains the most explicit Cyrus oracle in Scripture. YHWH calls Cyrus 'my anointed' (māšîaḥ, v.1) — the only non-Israelite to receive this title. YHWH promises to subdue nations, open doors, and give Cyrus treasures so that he will know there is no God but YHWH (v.3). The theological audacity is stunning: Israel's deliverer will be a Persian who does not acknowledge Israel's God. Yet YHWH uses even those who do not know him (v.4–5). The chapter's monotheistic affirmations ('I am YHWH, there is no other') are among the clearest in the Hebrew Bible.

sections.header: Verses 14–25 — “Turn to Me and Be Saved, All You Ends of the Earth”

heb.paragraph: "Turn to me and be saved (yēshaʿ), all you ends of the earth; for I am God, and there is no other" (v.22). The products of Egypt, Cush, and the Sabeans will come over to Israel in chains, acknowledging: "Surely God is with you, and there is no other" (v.14). "Truly you are a God who has been hiding himself, the God and Saviour of Israel" (v.15). Then the potter-clay exchange: "Does the clay say to the potter, ‘What are you making?’" (v.9) — Paul echoes this in Romans 9:20. "I have not spoken in secret, from somewhere in a land of darkness" (v.19). God’s revelation is public, not occult. Then the global invitation: "Turn to me and be saved, ALL you ends of the earth" (v.22). "Before me every knee will bow; by me every tongue will swear" (v.23) — Paul quotes this in Philippians 2:10–11 and Romans 14:11 for Christ.

notes.note: "Truly you are a God who hides himself." The hiddenness of God: working through Cyrus who doesn’t know him, through exile that looks like abandonment. The hidden God is the saving God.
"I have not spoken in secret. I, the LORD, speak the truth; I declare what is right." The contrast with pagan oracles that speak in riddles and darkness. God’s word is public and clear.

"Turn to me and be saved, all you ends of the earth. Before me every knee will bow; by me every tongue will swear." Phil 2:10–11 applies this to Jesus: "at the name of Jesus every knee should bow." Rom 14:11 quotes it for final judgment. The universal invitation: ALL the earth. The universal submission: EVERY knee.

refs.note: Paul quotes Isaiah 45:23 in his argument that all will give account to God — the universal-acknowledgment motif.
The Christ hymn applies the every-knee-bowing oracle to Jesus, making explicit the Christological reading.
The song of the Lamb echoes Isaiah's vision of all nations coming to worship the only true God.
The light-to-nations theme continues in the Second Servant Song, expanding salvation's scope beyond Israel.

hist.context: The universal-salvation oracle (vv.22–25) is programmatic: 'Turn to me and be saved, all you ends of the earth, for I am God and there is no other.' Every knee will bow, every tongue swear allegiance. This is not merely Israel's restoration but a vision of worldwide worship. The chapter also contains a theodicy-like dialogue (vv.9–13): the potter-and-clay imagery answers those who question YHWH's use of a pagan king. The Creator who formed the earth and gave it meaning does not owe explanations to creatures who are like potsherds among potsherds.

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIV“Woe to those who quarrel with their Maker, those who are nothing but potsherds among the potsherds on the ground. Does the clay say to the potter, ‘What are you making?’ Does your work say, ‘The potter has no hands’?ESV“Woe to him who strives with him who formed him, a pot among earthen pots! Does the clay say to him who forms it, ‘What are you making?’ or ‘Your work has no handles’?NIVFor the sake of Jacob my servant, of Israel my chosen, I summon you by name and bestow on you a title of honor, though you do not acknowledge me.ESVFor the sake of my servant Jacob, and Israel my chosen, I call you by your name, I name you, though you do not know me.

rec.text: ""To his anointed, to Cyrus." A pagan king called Messiah. The title reserved for Davidic kings given to a Persian emperor. The theological scandal is deliberate: God’s purposes exceed Israel’s categor…"

rows.label: Every Knee Will Bow — 45:14–25
rows.text: "Turn to me, ALL you ends of the earth!" "Before me EVERY KNEE will bow" (Phil 2:10; Rom 14:11). The hidden God.

hebtext.note: "This is what the LORD says to his anointed (māshîḥa), to Cyrus, whose right hand I take hold of to subdue nations before him" (v.1). Cyrus is called māshîḥa — MESSIAH. The only non-Israelite in the entire Bible to receive this title. "I summon you by name and bestow on you a title of honour, though you do not acknowledge me" (v.4). God uses people who don’t know him for purposes they don’t understand. "I am the LORD, and there is no other; apart from me there is no God. I will strengthen you, though you have not acknowledged me" (v.5). "I form the light and create darkness, I bring prosperity and create disaster; I, the LORD, do all these things" (v.7). The most comprehensive sovereignty claim in the Bible: light AND darkness, prosperity AND disaster.

"Turn to me and be saved (yēshaʿ), all you ends of the earth; for I am God, and there is no other" (v.22). The products of Egypt, Cush, and the Sabeans will come over to Israel in chains, acknowledging: "Surely God is with you, and there is no other" (v.14). "Truly you are a God who has been hiding himself, the God and Saviour of Israel" (v.15). Then the potter-clay exchange: "Does the clay say to the potter, ‘What are you making?’" (v.9) — Paul echoes this in Romans 9:20. "I have not spoken in secret, from somewhere in a land of darkness" (v.19). God’s revelation is public, not occult. Then the global invitation: "Turn to me and be saved, ALL you ends of the earth" (v.22). "Before me every knee will bow; by me every tongue will swear" (v.23) — Paul quotes this in Philippians 2:10–11 and Romans 14:11 for Christ.

themes.note: Sovereignty AND Mission peak at 10: Cyrus called Messiah. "I form light and create darkness." "Every knee will bow" (Phil 2:10). The universal invitation: all the earth. The most comprehensive sovereignty claim in the Bible.

coaching.tip: God calls Cyrus 'my anointed' — mashiach, the same term applied to David (v. 1). A pagan Persian emperor receives Israel's most sacred royal title. Isaiah makes the scandalous claim that God works through rulers who don't even know him (v. 4). This shatters any theology that limits God's action to the covenant community. Divine sovereignty is wider than religious boundaries.

'Turn to me and be saved, all you ends of the earth, for I am God, and there is no other' (v. 22). Paul quotes this in Romans 14:11 and Philippians 2:10–11 to describe universal acknowledgment of Christ. Isaiah's monotheistic universalism — one God for all nations — becomes the theological foundation for the Gentile mission. The most exclusive claim (no other God) produces the most inclusive invitation (all ends of the earth).

prayer_prompt: As God calls Cyrus by name before he is born and declares 'Turn to me and be saved, all you ends of the earth,' consider asking God where His sovereign plan is unfolding in places and people you would never expect.
